userRequest: Create an implementation plan for issue #638: hooks-mux: add defer/block/retry decisions + updatedInput mutation. This depends on foundational work being implemented in parallel.

include: Add defer, block, and retry as first-class hooks-mux decisions with explicit merge precedence and diagnostics.
Map Claude updatedInput at the adapter boundary to one canonical internal mutation model, preferably existing toolMutation unless runtime tracing proves otherwise.
Render Claude defer/block/retry outputs only for supported event contracts and explicitly test degraded behavior where unsupported.
Wire agent-platform consumers for block, bounded retry, continue:false session abort, sessionTitle propagation, and updatedInput/toolMutation application.
Handle watchPaths only if runtime daemon watcher APIs can safely register dynamic paths; otherwise document and test the explicit deferral.
Update stale hook gap documentation after implementation.
exclude: Do not implement missing hook events from #636 except where already landed interfaces must be consumed.
Do not implement new handler types from #637 except where already landed interfaces must be consumed.
Do not reimplement output fields that are already supported unless end-to-end propagation tests expose a real gap.

qualityGates: Runtime trace lists live call paths from hook handler result through merge, adapter rendering, and agent-platform consumers before implementation.
Failing tests are authored before implementation and cover every accepted #638 criterion.
No duplicate canonical mutation model is introduced without explicit justification; updatedInput and toolMutation compatibility is tested.
Decision precedence is deterministic and backward compatible for existing allow, deny, ask, continue, and noop behavior.
Retry behavior is bounded, auditable, and cannot silently re-execute unsafe effects forever.
watchPaths is either implemented end to end with daemon tests or explicitly documented as deferred with tests preventing false support.
Documentation reflects corrected current support and remaining gaps.

parallelDependencyPolicy.defaultAction: Proceed only against stable landed interfaces. If #636 or #637 changes handler result or event surfaces in incompatible ways, raise a breakpoint before implementation.
parallelDependencyPolicy.compatibilityGuidance: Prefer a small adapter seam over duplicating foundational work. Delete any temporary compatibility code if the dependency lands before final acceptance.
